strengths: 19+ years of hands-on software engineering across web, embedded, low-latency backend, and full-stack systems — exceeds the 15-year minimum
Entrepreneurial track record: founder of GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform running on Kubernetes) sustained since 2016
Current work on AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic-workflow frameworks shows recent, relevant AI tooling experience
concerns: No recognized AI expertise or shipped AI products/platforms at scale — current AI work is recent (2025) and at contributor level
Career ceiling is Senior/Staff IC; no evidence of director-level scope, strategic advisory to customers, or organization-wide technical influence
No Master's or PhD in an AI-related field, and no cloud platform strategy experience (AWS CDK usage is tactical, not strategic)

key_factors.factor: Recognized AI expertise and AI product leadership
Experience tenure and breadth (19 years, meets minimum)

response.justification: The candidate clearly meets the minimum bar: a CS bachelor's degree and roughly 19 years of engineering experience spanning low-latency D services at Dunnhumby (millions of requests/sec), full-stack TypeScript product work, and embedded/hardware exposure at National Instruments. The entrepreneurial preference is partly satisfied by founding GISCollective and conference speaking (FOSS4G 2019). However, this is a Director-level AI strategy role requiring recognized AI expertise with demonstrated AI-built products; the résumé shows only recent (since April 2025) contributor-level work on MCP generators and agent tooling, no advanced AI degree, no evidence of advising strategic customers, and no leadership beyond Staff IC. The core competency gap — AI thought leadership and strategic influence at scale — is unmet, so despite strong engineering fundamentals the fit for this specific role is weak.
